channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How to Scale Your Engineering Team from 10 to 100
item.description: March 06, 2026 / Mikael Danielian Most engineering leaders hit a wall somewhere between 10 and 30 people. What worked when you could hold the whole team in one standup stops working — fast. The...

item.title: How a 2% Latency Spike Collapses a 20-Service System and How to Prevent It
item.description: Last week, we modeled cascading database connection pool exhaustion in a distributed microservices architecture. No servers were killed. No regions failed. No database crashed. But the system...

item.title: NestJS 마이크로서비스에서 SSE 스트리밍 추상화하기 — Redis Pub/Sub 기반 3-Tier Base Class 설계
item.description: NestJS 마이크로서비스에서 SSE 스트리밍 추상화하기 — Redis Pub/Sub 기반 3-Tier Base Class 설계 배경 / 문제 상황 MSA 환경에서 실시간 이벤트를 클라이언트에 전달해야 했다. 우리 서비스는 ECS 위에 여러 인스턴스가 뜨고, 이벤트를 발행하는 마이크로서비스와 SSE를 내려보내는 Gateway가 물리적으로 다른...

item.title: Solved: Bundle Size Investigation: A Step-by-Step Guide to Shrinking Your JavaScript
item.description: 🚀 Executive Summary TL;DR: Bloated JavaScript bundles lead to slow web app performance and poor user experience, often stemming from excessive dependency imports and inefficient loading. This...

item.title: Spot the Savings 🎯 Run Non-Critical Workloads on Azure Spot VMs and Save Up to 90%
item.description: Your CI runners, batch jobs, and dev environments don't need guaranteed availability. Azure Spot VMs use unused capacity at up to 90% discount. Here's how to deploy them safely with Terraform,...

item.title: The Self-Contract Pattern: Why Scalable AI Agents Have Three Required Files
item.description: Most AI agents fail the same way: they drift. Not dramatically. Gradually. The agent that was crisp and reliable on day one starts hallucinating its own scope on day thirty. It tries things it...

item.title: Turn Telegram into your AI Terminal: Introducing Telegram Coding Bridge (Now Open Source!)
item.description: Have you ever needed to quickly drop a file onto your local machine, restart a dev server, or even ask an AI to refactor a file while you're away from your desk? I just open-sourced Telegram...

item.title: Claude Code를 팀의 시니어 개발자로 만들기: .claude 디렉토리 설계 전략
item.description: 배경 / 문제 상황 AI 코딩 도구를 사용하다 보면 한 가지 불만이 생긴다. 매번 같은 규칙을 알려줘야 한다는 것. "Entity에는 반드시 comment를 넣어줘", "DTO에는 ApiProperty 빼먹지 마", "import 순서는 이렇게 해"... 프로젝트 컨벤션을 매번 프롬프트에 넣는 건 비효율적이다. Claude Code의 .claude...

item.title: Solved: Are we ignoring the main source of AI cost? Not the GPU price, but wasted training & serving minutes.
item.description: 🚀 Executive Summary TL;DR: The true cost of AI stems from wasted training and serving minutes, not GPU hardware prices, often due to a ‘fire and forget’ mentality. Solutions range from quick ‘Dead...

item.title: How I'd Build an AI Agent to Predict the T20 World Cup 2026
item.description: How I'd Build an AI Agent to Predict the T20 World Cup 2026 Twenty teams. Spinning dustbowls in Kandy. Flat batting decks in Mumbai. The ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 is heading to India and Sri...

item.title: A roadmap for AI, if anyone will listen
item.description: The Pro-Human Declaration was finalized before last week's Pentagon-Anthropic standoff, but the collision of the two events wasn’t lost on anyone involved.

item.title: Google just gave Sundar Pichai a $692M pay package
item.description: Most of it is tied to performance, including new stock incentives linked to Waymo and Wing, its drone delivery venture.

item.title: Grammarly’s ‘expert review’ is just missing the actual experts
item.description: A recently-added feature in Grammarly purports to improve users’ writing with help from the world's great writers and thinkers — and some tech journalists, too.

item.title: OpenAI robotics lead Caitlin Kalinowski quits in response to Pentagon deal
item.description: Hardware executive Caitlin Kalinowski announced today that in response to OpenAI's controversial agreement with the Department of Defense, she’s resigned from her role leading the company's...

item.title: Sony appears to be testing dynamic pricing on PlayStation games
item.description: A site called PSprices has been tracking prices on Sony's digital game store and noticed something unusual: some games were being offered at different prices to different users. What's more, those...

item.title: OpenAI delays ChatGPT’s ‘adult mode’ again
item.description: The feature, which will give verified adult users access to erotica and other adult content, had already been delayed from December.

item.title: Time’s running out to get a free gift card when you preorder a new MacBook
item.description: This has been a week to remember here at The Verge. MWC 2026 in Barcelona lead straight into Apple’s week of product announcements. There was truly something for everyone, whether you’ve been...

item.title: OpenAI Codex Security Scanned 1.2 Million Commits and Found 10,561 High-Severity Issues
item.description: OpenAI on Friday began rolling out Codex Security, an artificial intelligence (AI)-powered security agent that's designed to find, validate, and propose fixes for vulnerabilities. The feature is...

item.title: Vizio accounts are becoming Walmart accounts
item.description: Walmart bought Vizio in 2024, and now it's taking the next steps to formally fold the TV maker into itself by phasing out Vizio accounts. Now, when you purchase a new Vizio TV, you'll be asked to...

item.title: The OpenClaw superfan meetup serves optimism and lobster
item.description: The woman at the door wore a plush lobster headdress. She sat in the front hallway of a multistory event venue in Manhattan, beside a bundle of wristbands. If she granted you one, the world of...

item.title: A bite-sized adventure that puts a wrench into the classic Zelda formula
item.description: There are a lot of games that try to emulate The Legend of Zelda, but few that manage to capture that spirit in such a small, concise package as Ratcheteer DX. The postapocalyptic game only takes...

item.title: Apple’s cheap laptop looks like a winner
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 118, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, please take my Switch away so I can get some work done, and also...

item.title: The Corvette ZR1X hybrid can outpace million-dollar sports cars for a fraction of the cost
item.description: When Elon Musk burst onto the scene in his little Tesla Roadster, it seemed a matter of time before electricity rendered gas-powered sports cars obsolete. It hasn't worked out that way. Automakers...

item.title: Anthropic Finds 22 Firefox Vulnerabilities Using Claude Opus 4.6 AI Model
item.description: Anthropic on Friday said it discovered 22 new security vulnerabilities in the Firefox web browser as part of a security partnership with Mozilla. Of these, 14 have been classified as high, seven...

item.title: DJI will pay $30K to the man who accidentally hacked 7,000 Romo robovacs
item.description: On Valentine's Day, I brought you a story that's since made headlines all around the world: How one man, just trying to steer his DJI robot vacuum with a PlayStation gamepad, discovered an entire...

item.title: This Jammer Wants to Block Always-Listening AI Wearables. It Probably Won’t Work
item.description: Deveillance’s Spectre I, developed by a recent Harvard grad, wants to give people control over the always-on wearables surrounding their lives. The problem? Physics.

item.title: Valve says it still plans to ship the Steam Machine in 2026
item.description: A blog post from Valve on Friday initially seemed to throw cold water on the idea that the Steam Machine, Steam Frame, and Steam Controller would arrive in 2026 at all. But Valve tells The Verge...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid in Byzantine Monk Cooking
item.description: This is a very weird story about how squid stayed on the menu of Byzantine monks by falling between the cracks of dietary rules. At Constantinople’s Monastery of Stoudios, the kitchen didn’t...

item.title: Grammarly is using our identities without permission
item.description: Grammarly's "expert review" feature offers to give users writing advice "inspired by" subject matter experts, including recently deceased professors, as Wired reported on Wednesday. When I tried...

item.title: Microsoft, Google, Amazon say Anthropic Claude remains available to non-defense customers
item.description: Trump's Department of War feud with Anthropic won't impact other companies that are using Claude via Microsoft and Google products.
